steps.title: Map the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The first sentence calls the statues "extremely heavy." The sentence with the blank follows with a specific weight figure (each weighing several tons). The second sentence is not introducing new information or a contrast — it is proving the prior claim with concrete evidence. The transition must signal confirmation or elaboration.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
Work through each option:
  • Choice A — 'In any case,': signals a concession or a conclusion regardless of prior context — not evidence supporting a specific claim. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Earlier,': signals a time shift to a past moment. The weight is a present fact, not a prior event. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In fact,': signals that the following statement confirms or strengthens the previous one with a more specific detail. The tonnage figure proves "extremely heavy." ✓
  • Choice D — 'Instead,': signals substitution or contradiction. The weight does not replace or contradict the prior claim. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
